text: When designing REST APIs, there are several best practices to follow. First, your endpoints should be clear and based on nouns, not verbs, such as `/users` or `/products`. Always use appropriate HTTP status codes like 200 for success and 404 for not found. It is essential to include thorough documentation so that developers understand how to use your API correctly. Also, implement versioning in your API, for example by adding `/v1/` to the URL path, to avoid breaking existing applications when you make updates. Following these guidelines makes your API consistent, reliable, and easier to work with.
